content: Sebastien asked for board deck outline by EOW. ARR $3.4M vs $3.8M target. Told him we're documenting the miss in customer retention, not hiding it.
Board members: Sequoia partner (lead), Accel, founder reserves. Series A deferred to Q2 pending SOC2 and unit econ clarity.
summary: Board composition and Series A timeline

content: Anya flagged VAR model assumes 110% net retention; actual is 94%. Needs reframing in deck as 'cohort stabilization underway.' Acme churn spike still unexplained.
summary: NRR 94% vs model assumption; Acme churn issue

content: Lina presented roadmap: Q1 embedding caching, Q2 multi-agent features. Board will push for timeline on enterprise features. Told her to expect 'why not now' questions.
summary: Q1–Q2 roadmap review; enterprise features pressure

content: Board deck template: 10 slides max. Slide 1 = mission/traction, 2–3 = metrics, 4 = unit econ, 5 = risks, 6 = hiring plan, 7 = cap table, 8 = ask. Always lead with what changed since last update.
summary: Clude board deck structure and rhythm

content: SOC2 audit on track for March 20. Cloudflare and DataDog configs reviewed. One Sentry access policy needs tightening but low risk.
summary: SOC2 Type II audit nearly complete

content: Hiring: James (Senior Backend) started Mar 3. Tom ramping on service auth. Priya says Q1 target (3 hires) still achievable if we close 1 design candidate by end of month.
summary: Q1 hiring 2/3 complete; design slot open

content: Marcus reported Wayne Industries POC stalling. Initech moving forward. Globex contract review by legal—no blockers yet. Asked Marcus to get POC outcomes doc to Sebastien by Wed for board context.
Ari flagged Voyage AI integration delays (their API changes). Pushed back delivery from late Q1 to mid-Q2. Board will ask—prepping 'supplier dependency' slide for deck.
summary: Voyage AI integration slips; Q2 now

content: I hate re-explaining burn and runway to investors who should've read the last update. This deck needs to be 'what changed,' not a repeat. If Sebastien wants narrative, it goes in speaker notes, not slides.
summary: Investor comms pet peeve; prioritize delta

content: Deck draft complete. Sebastien wants three scenarios: base case ($4.2M ARR by EOY), upside (early Series A traction), downside (extended runway, reduced hiring). Spending tomorrow red-teaming the base case with Anya.
Clude's investor thesis: reduce RAG hallucination through fine-grained memory updates. Board cares about TAM clarity and why we own it vs Anthropic or Voyage.
summary: Core investor pitch and competitive moat

content: Jenny delivered new brand materials for board deck. Sequoia partner likes modern look. Asked for one edit: remove the 'cognitive agents' tag line—too buzzwordy. Reframed as 'reliable agent memory.'
Acme Corp churn: Maya reached out directly. Their use case shifted post-acquisition. Offered free pilot on new workload. Sebastien aware—not hiding from board, but also not leading with it.
summary: Acme churn root cause + remediation plan

content: Board deck final review with Sebastien, Anya, Lina. Q: why not push harder on enterprise sales? A: Initech and Globex deals still ramping; Wayne Industries needs post-pilot win before we scale CS. Deck notes this dependency.
summary: Board readiness; enterprise sales reality check

content: Board meeting March 22. Sequoia partner asked about Anthropic partnership—clarified we're a customer, not a dependent. Series A conversation moved to May pending Q2 metrics. Retention trending up (good signal for next update).
summary: Board meeting recap; Series A to May; retention improving
